selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"As a cleaner who spends only a few times monthly on social media, I understand the frustration of encountering misleading or false information online. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a cleaner who spends only a few times monthly on social media, I understand the frustration of encountering misleading or false information online. Fake content can be not just misleading but also harmful, especially if it influences decisions or spreads panic. To manage this effectively, I believe starting by being more selective with what I engage with is essential. I often skim headlines and avoid clicking on posts from accounts or pages I don’t know well. This helps reduce the chances of stumbling upon fake news or misleading content. Additionally, I rely on fact-checking tools or credible sources that I trust to verify information before sharing or reacting to it online.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: My persona traits, particularly my low extraversion and high conscientiousness, suggest a methodical approach to managing social media. My low openness score means I might not actively seek out diverse perspectives, so I can limit exposure by curating my feeds carefully. My high conscientiousness drives me toward being cautious with what I consume and share online. The fact-checking tools align with my natural tendency to verify information thoroughly before acting or reacting. By combining these traits—avoidance of ambiguity, reliability, and analytical thinking—I can navigate social media more safely and effectively.
